title: Delegation of Authority for Entitlement Extensions Beyond 48 Months
impact: Officially delegated the concurrence authority for Chapter 31 entitlement extensions exceeding 48 months from the Central Office down to regional VR&E Officers (VREOs) and Assistant VREOs.
details: Eliminated the bureaucratic bottleneck of sending extension requests to Washington D.C., allowing regional offices to approve extensions directly.

title: Modifications to Retroactive Induction Approvals and GI Bill Reinstatement
impact: Clarified that retroactive induction can cover periods of self-pay or Chapter 33 Post-9/11 GI Bill usage prior to the formal application date if the veteran was entitled.
details: Hardened the rules ensuring veterans are reimbursed for past training costs if service-connected disability was active and unaccommodated during that period.

title: Agency Information Collection Activity: Pre- and Post-Independent Living Assessment
impact: Authorized and standardized the use of VA Form 28-0791 for pre- and post-rehabilitation independent living assessments, focusing on home modifications and activities of daily living.
details: Standardized the objective evaluation of independent living limitations using a structured clinical assessment form.
